text.title: 2 days in Osaka, Japan | 48 Hours of Eating, Shopping, Cafes & City Views | Japan Travel VLOG
text.u: Hi everyone, today we’re headed over to Osaka
grabbing a coffee for the train ride
today I’m taking the Shinkansen from Shinagawa station
I thought it would be a bit less confusing than Tokyo station
it’s quite a early train so needed the extra shot
also getting something to eat
and we’re off!
we’ll be in Osaka for about 3 days
I mostly wanted to visit USJ during halloween season
but decided to stay a few extra days
I got the chicken and egg bento
I’ve been to Osaka quite recently
so I don’t really have set plans
we’re just going to eat and explore the city :)
just dropped off our bags
now let’s go look around
I usually like to stay near Umeda
which is less busy than Namba
Umeda still has all the major shops and restaurants
I wanted to ride the big ferris wheel at Hep 5
but it’s under construction until April next year :(
so we’re having a change of plans
to Namba
time for some lunch
there’s so many options for food
we decided on some Kushikatsu
which is this deep-fried skewers originating from Osaka
the benishouga (pickled ginger) is my favorite
the mozzarella stick was also so good
one thing I think is iconic to Osaka
are all these eccentric shop signs
which you can find all along Dotombori
it’s fun to go around looking at the signs
although Dotombori is busy and crowded
I quite like the lively atmosphere
the main streets are lined with these souvenir shops
this mascot is called Kuidaore Taro
he was originally a mascot for a local restaurant
and now an iconic symbol in Osaka
his name means eat till you drop
which is reflective of the city’s foodie culture
of course there are tons of other souvenirs as well
they still had a few of the Expo mascots
since we couldn’t get on the Hep 5 ferris wheel
we decided to try the one at donki
I’ve walked past it a few times but never been on it
I didn’t know the signs actually moved too
the windows are a bit scratched
but you get a pretty decent view from up top
it does get a bit rocky at the top
might not be for those who are scared of heights
checking out what gachas are in
they change up all the time so it’s always fun to look
collecting mini camera :)
I really want the one with the two lenses
and so we go again...
this is how they hook you in
and I finally got it on the 3rd round
we’re back in Umeda
we weren’t quite satisfied with the ferris wheel view
so we decided to go to another observatory
I have to say this one might be my favorite
it’s also the best place to see the sunset
I suggest coming about an hour before sunset
as there tends to be quite a long line to get in during this time
but I think the view is definitely worth it
and we made it just in time
you can actually see the sun go down
and there is also a outside viewing platform
so you can see the actual sun set
and it’s the end of another day
I like that you can see the river and the city from here
and it was such a lovely sunset today
I like watching the city go by
it’s kind of peaceful to see from above
time to head back down
tonights dinner is robatayaki
which is this way of cooking over hot charcoal
you can choose anything from the display
and they grill it fresh for you on the charcoal
usually include vegetable, seafood and meats
and everyone sits around the grill
so you can watch the food being prepared
and they hand it to you straight from the grill
best enjoyed with a cold beer
the grilled yam was one of my favorites
the scallops were also a must
it does get quite busy so I recommend making a reservation online
we’re back at Nakazakicho today
this area is filled with small cafes and shops
choose this cafe because it had pretty good food options
and a cute design
the second floor had a nice sitting area
the queue started to get quite long right after we got in
I suggest coming around opening to avoid the wait
now let’s have a walk around
I came here last time I was in Osaka too
it’s such a nice neighborhood to just walk around
a lot of the shops were closed last time I came
they tend to open in the afternoon
so I didn’t get to see any of the shops last time
there’s also a lot of vintage stores here
with some really unique and fun items
found some really cute plant pots
this shop also had some really cute handmade items
I regret not getting these
I think they’ll make such a unique souvenir
this had to be one of my favorite stores
this is one of the vintage stores
there’s not only clothes but tons of fun retro items
we had a lot of fun just looking around
there’s a lot of items from the 80s
so you can find some nostalgic pieces too
time for another cafe
this cosy cafe is located in a old house
and it also turns into bar at night
the atmosphere is so warm and cosy
they’re most popular for their chai
their drinks are a bit watered down
but a good place to relax and chill
there’s one more spot we want to visit today
which is about 40 minutes from Umeda
this bookstore has a massive collection of over 150,000 books
with this unique floor-wide layout
this store is actually the birthplace of Tsutaya
the entire Tsutaya concept originated here in 1983
store embodies lifestyles through books, music, and media
definitely a shop for all book lovers
the first floor is the actual bookstore
I love looking through photo and travel books to get ideas
found more miniature cameras
each Tsutaya has different items
it’s not only books but you can find stationary and lifestyle goods
the 2026 calendars are already out
the third floor has clothes and food
love all the cute packaging
there’s also a section selling kitchenware
time to head back
there’s still quite a bit of time before our train
so we’re just looking around Osaka station
I have so many fun travel plans coming up
I can’t wait to share it with you all soon
we’re having yakiniku for dinner
this place had all you can eat yakiniku for 4,000 yen
and all the meat were really good
a perfect end to the trip
